title: Wichuraea tubulosa
intro: Wichuraea tubulosa, also known as the "Mangrove fern," is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical mangrove forests of Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Wichuraea tubulosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Wichuraea tubulosa belongs to the family Diapensiaceae. It is a species of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Asia.
